openQuestions: What effects will the preregistered live policy-by-helper benchmark observe across model families?
What is the privacy-safe natural request distribution for each hub?
resolvedQuestions: What is the strongest evidence-backed case that named defaults remain preferable, and where does the run-1 verdict overreach?
How should named-versus-null policy and fallback-resource shape be evaluated in a runnable live benchmark without mutating shipped hubs?
What exact third-archetype schema, migration order, rollback telemetry, and benchmark contracts would make policy changes safe?
Which cross-hub edge cases break a binary named-default-versus-null policy?
What second-order anti-patterns emerge from null routing, mode-map loading, contextual defaults, or multi-dominant modes?
keyFindings: Zero-signal replay leaves workflowMode null while defaultApplied reports only that a named default is configured.
sk-prompt supports a bounded named recommendation, but repository fixtures do not prove an 80 percent traffic share.
CLI and MCP gold scenarios explicitly require defer despite configured named defaults.
A paired live named-versus-null benchmark is needed to distinguish harmless metadata from behavior-shaping bias.
A 2x4 policy-by-helper fixture matrix isolates named-versus-null behavior from fallback-resource shape.
A balanced ambiguity corpus, blind evidence grading, and repeated blocked sampling make live results auditable.
Full registry, compressed card, child hint, and no-helper arms test distinct routing and token-cost hypotheses.
Selection policy must be separated from optional recommendation metadata through keyword-default, defer, and detect archetypes.
defaultApplied requires dual-write compatibility as defaultConsulted, not a direct rename to defaultConfigured.
Safe rollout adds tolerant readers, telemetry, fixtures, canon, annotations, and gates before any hub policy flip.
Null routing has distinct defer and detection contracts and can still bias, starve, or fan out without validated neutral fallback behavior.
ruledOutDirections: Treating routeTelemetry.defaultApplied as evidence that replay selected the default child.
Using deterministic replay alone to settle live-model behavior.
A simple named-versus-null A/B test with one shared helper.
Treating the full registry as the automatic control winner.
Using one live run per case.
Inferring the routing archetype from defaultMode nullability.
Encoding contextual or multi-dominant defaults as static recommendation metadata.
Flipping shipped hubs before compatibility readers, telemetry, fixtures, and gates exist.

saturatedDirections: Re-deriving the run-1 per-hub verdict without new evidence
Treating deterministic replay telemetry as live child selection
Using a confounded two-arm policy experiment
